---
title: "Methodology"
toc: true
---
## Entity Resolution as Research Methodology

CORE approaches entity resolution as a scientific methodology rather than as a standalone matching algorithm.

The framework integrates computational procedures, scientific judgement, expert review, and knowledge preservation into a coherent methodological system that supports transparent and reproducible empirical research.

From this perspective, entity resolution extends beyond identifying correspondences between records.

It includes:

- data preparation;
- computational evaluation;
- expert review;
- knowledge preservation;
- production of analysis-ready datasets.

Each of these activities contributes to the scientific validity of empirical research.

---

# Purpose

CORE was developed to support empirical research by combining computational efficiency with transparent scientific judgement.

The objective is not simply to maximise matching performance.

Instead, the framework seeks to ensure that entity resolution decisions become:

- explicit;
- reproducible;
- reviewable;
- reusable;
- scientifically accountable.

Entity resolution therefore becomes a documented component of the research process rather than an undocumented preprocessing task.

---

# Methodological Principles

CORE is built around five methodological principles.

## Transparency

Every important methodological decision remains observable and open to scientific inspection.

Researchers are able to inspect intermediate outputs, review decisions, and understand how final correspondences were established.

---

## Scientific Judgement

Computational procedures support expert reasoning but do not replace it whenever uncertainty remains.

Scientific judgement therefore remains an explicit component of the framework rather than an undocumented correction performed outside the software.

---

## Reproducibility

Entity resolution decisions are documented in a manner that allows independent verification and future replication.

Reproducibility within CORE extends beyond producing identical analytical datasets.

The framework seeks to preserve the reasoning through which those datasets were constructed.

---

## Knowledge Preservation

Validated scientific judgement is preserved beyond individual projects so that future investigations may benefit from previous empirical evaluation.

Knowledge generated during entity resolution therefore becomes a reusable scientific asset rather than a temporary by-product of data preparation.

---

## Methodological Continuity

Entity resolution is regarded as a cumulative research activity in which validated knowledge contributes to successive investigations rather than being repeatedly reconstructed.

Scientific knowledge therefore accumulates across projects rather than disappearing at the conclusion of individual studies.

---

# Computational Evidence and Scientific Judgement

CORE combines computational evidence with scientific judgement.

Neither component alone is sufficient for reliable entity resolution in all circumstances.

The framework therefore integrates:

```text
Computational Evidence
            +
Scientific Judgement
            +
Knowledge Preservation
            ↓
Transparent Entity Resolution
```

Computational procedures provide consistency and scalability.

Scientific judgement provides contextual understanding and methodological interpretation.

Knowledge preservation ensures that validated conclusions remain available for future empirical research.

---

# Reproducibility

Within CORE, reproducibility extends beyond obtaining identical analytical datasets.

The methodology seeks to preserve the reasoning through which entity resolution decisions are established, allowing future researchers to inspect, evaluate, and reproduce the methodological process itself.

Computational procedures contribute reproducibility by applying consistent rules across observations.

Scientific judgement contributes reproducibility by remaining documented, transparent, and open to future evaluation.

Reproducibility is therefore understood as the ability to reconstruct both analytical outputs and the methodological pathway through which those outputs were produced.

---

# Scientific Outputs

The primary output of CORE is not limited to an analysis-ready dataset.

The methodology produces multiple forms of scientific output, including:

- validated entity resolution decisions;
- documented expert judgement;
- preserved methodological evidence;
- reusable scientific knowledge;
- reproducible analytical workflows.

These outputs complement the analytical dataset by providing transparency regarding how empirical observations have been interpreted and connected.

Consequently, entity resolution becomes a documented research activity whose methodological products remain available for future scientific investigation rather than disappearing at the conclusion of individual projects.

---

# Learning Philosophy

Knowledge preserved through the Learning Layer does not automatically influence future workflows.

A single reviewer may make mistakes.

Project-specific assumptions may later prove incorrect.

New evidence may challenge earlier conclusions.

CORE therefore distinguishes three stages of knowledge integration.

## Passive Learning

Validated decisions are preserved but do not influence computational procedures.

## Consensus Learning

Knowledge becomes eligible for methodological influence only after sufficient independent scientific validation has accumulated.

## Soft Learning

Validated knowledge influences future workflows through transparent score adjustments and review prioritisation rather than automatic acceptance or rejection.

This approach protects future investigations from isolated reviewer errors while preserving the cumulative development of scientific knowledge.

---

# Methodological Perspective

CORE approaches entity resolution as a scientific methodology rather than as a standalone computational task.

The framework integrates computational procedures, scientific judgement, expert review, and knowledge preservation into a coherent methodological system that supports transparent and reproducible empirical research.

Each component contributes a distinct responsibility, while together they establish a continuous process through which uncertainty is evaluated, knowledge is validated, and scientific understanding is preserved.

From this perspective, the purpose of entity resolution extends beyond constructing analytical datasets.

It becomes a process through which empirical knowledge is generated, documented, preserved, and made available for future research.

---

# Final Principle

> Entity resolution does not merely produce analytical datasets.
>
> It produces scientific knowledge.

The methodology implemented within CORE has been designed around this principle.
